artifacts.content:
From: Amanda Leigh Rodriguez <amanda.rodriguez@bluesageequestrian.com>
To: Mustang Heritage Foundation Adoption Committee <info@mustangheritage.org>
Cc: Jessica Thompson <jessica.thompson@frontrangeha.com>
Date: 2025-03-11 14:26:09 -0700
Subject: Reference for Jessica Thompson – Trainer/Adopter Applicant

Dear Adoption Committee,

I’m writing to provide a reference for Jessica Marie Thompson, who listed me as a trainer/competitor contact on her mustang adopter application.

I’ve known Jessica for just over 7 years, since February 2019, when we both ended up in the same small-claims mediation at the Larimer County Courthouse over separate boarding contract disputes with Willow Creek Stables. We started talking in the hallway about our horses, realized we shared similar training philosophies, and ended up moving our geldings together to the County Road 19 barn a few weeks later. We’ve been close friends and professional collaborators ever since.

In that time, I’ve watched Jessica bring along multiple BLM mustangs and a handful of previously mishandled horses at Blue Sage Equestrian here in Berthoud, CO. She is exceptionally patient, safety-conscious, and thoughtful about matching each horse’s temperament and physical needs with an appropriate workload. Her current string includes her mare Juniper and a rehab mustang gelding, and she keeps meticulous notes on every ride.

Per your form, you asked for the adopter/competitor applicant’s phone number for follow-up. Jessica’s preferred contact is her cell: (970) 555-1842. Email is usually reliable as well: jessica.m.thompson@gmail.com.

Please feel free to call or email me if you’d like to discuss her skills and suitability in more detail.

Best regards,

Amanda Leigh Rodriguez
Assistant Trainer, Blue Sage Equestrian LLC
Cell: 970-555-7339
Berthoud, CO

artifacts.content:
From: Front Range Mustang Challenge <info@frmchallenge.org>
To: Jessica Thompson <jessica.thompson@example.com>
Subject: Incomplete Applicant Information – Phone Number Needed
Date: 2024-03-14 16:02:00 -0700

Dear Jessica,

Thank you for submitting your competitor application for the 2024 Front Range Mustang Challenge. We are excited to see another trainer from Blue Sage Equestrian in the mix.

During our preliminary review, we noticed that the section labeled “Adopter/Competitor Applicant’s Phone” is incomplete. The line currently lists only “see above,” but the application requires a clearly stated primary phone number where you can reliably be reached during business hours.

For our records, please reply to this email with the single best number for event staff and potential adopters to use. You may choose either:

1. Your personal cell (the 970 number you listed under emergency contacts), or
2. Your work line through FrontRange Health Analytics, which you wrote as 720-555-9924.

Once we receive confirmation of which number you prefer to have associated with your adopter/competitor profile, we will update your file manually and mark your application as complete. Please do not send multiple numbers; only one will be displayed on the public catalog card attached to your assigned mustang.

As a reminder, this phone will be used by prospective adopters to contact you during and after the event, so choose the one you monitor most closely. You do not need to resend the full application packet.

Best regards,

Kara Jensen
Volunteer Coordinator
Front Range Mustang Challenge
info@frmchallenge.org
303-555-4410

metadata.title: Call FR Mustang Challenge – Confirm Applicant Phone

artifacts.content:
From: Jessica Thompson <jess.thompson@frontrangehealthanalytics.com>
To: volunteerprogram@larimer4h.org
Cc: amanda.rodriguez.training@gmail.com
Date: 2025-02-11 08:17 MST
Subject: Riding/Training Reference for Amanda Rodriguez

Hi Sarah,

Thanks for reaching out about Amanda’s application to help with the 4-H green horse group this spring. I’m happy to be listed as a riding and character reference.

Amanda and I board and train together at Blue Sage Equestrian here in Berthoud. I usually see her ride or work horses 3–4 evenings a week, and during the intensive period last summer—roughly June through early September 2024—I was out there with her almost every day. My younger brother Ryan was staying with me after his DUI hearing in Larimer County, and I didn’t want to leave him alone at the house, so he’d hang out in the barn lounge while I watched Amanda help me start two new BLM mustangs under saddle.

In terms of skills, Amanda is very patient with nervous or previously mishandled horses and is good at explaining things to less experienced riders without talking down to them. She’s also reliable about safety—helmets, appropriate footwear, checking tack, etc.—which I know is important for your program.

If you need to follow up by phone, the best number to reach me is my cell, (303) 555-9274. I’m usually available after 9:30 a.m. on weekdays between project calls for my remote job, or most Sunday afternoons after we finish chores at the barn.

Let me know if you’d like more detail on specific horses or situations where I’ve seen Amanda work through issues.

Best,

Jessica Marie Thompson
Berthoud, CO
Part-time colt starter / horsemanship trainer
Remote Project Coordinator, FrontRange Health Analytics, Inc.

metadata.subject: Riding/Training Reference for Amanda Rodriguez

artifacts.content:
From: Amanda Rodriguez <arodriguez.trails@gmail.com>
To: Jessica Thompson <jess.thompson@frontrangehealth.com>
Date: 2024-06-11 08:17
Subject: Reference form & best number for follow-up

Hey Jess,

I just finished the first draft of the reference questionnaire for that colt-starting clinic application you’re submitting. They’ve got a whole section about “reference contact information,” including a line for my primary phone number.

I put my cell as **(970) 555-1832** for now, since that’s the one I actually answer when I’m at Blue Sage or hauling. If they insist on a landline, I can add the office number at the counseling center, but honestly I’m rarely near that phone.

One of the questions asked how long I’ve known you as a trainer, and I wrote that it’s been a little over seven years—since March 2019, when my therapist at Longs Peak Behavioral Health, Dr. Karen Ellison, suggested I start weekly lessons with you at Blue Sage as part of my PTSD treatment plan after the barn fire at Willow Creek Stables on County Road 21 where my previous gelding died. I hope that level of detail doesn’t feel like too much, but it seemed relevant to how I’ve seen you work with horses and humans.

Double-check that the email address they should use for me is still correct: I listed **arodriguez.trails@gmail.com** and my backup work email, **amanda.rodriguez@lpbehavioral.org**.

If they call at a weird time and I miss it, I’ll call back as soon as I’m out of session or off the rail.

Want to review my answers over coffee after chores tomorrow?

– Amanda

metadata.subject: Reference form & best number for follow-up

artifacts.content:
From: Evan Carter <evan.carter@gmail.com>
To: Jessica Thompson <jess.thompson@frontrangehealth.com>
Date: 2023-06-19 22:54
Subject: Late-night barn checks & that reference call

Jess,

Just got your text about staying at Blue Sage past 10:30 again to keep an eye on the new mustang gelding. I know you’re worried about him settling in, but I’m still not thrilled about you driving back and forth alone so late between the barn and the house, especially after that minor break-in on County Road 19 earlier this month.

Did the clinic coordinator ever follow up with your reference? Amanda said she gave them her cell, **(970) 555-1832**, as the best number to reach her. She also mentioned that one of the questions on the form was whether she had any concerns about the horses in your care, and she told them she didn’t—but that she *did* have concerns about you running yourself ragged and being on the road at midnight while I’m stuck on these 12-hour night shifts at Poudre Valley Hospital.

If they need a second reference contact number, you can list mine: **(720) 555-6640**. I’m in and out of service at work, but they can always leave a voicemail.

Please text when you leave the barn so I know you’re on the way. And consider skipping the 11:30 p.m. check tonight—the mustang will survive one less peek, and I’d rather you weren’t out there alone with that sketchy traffic on 287.

Love you,
Evan

metadata.subject: Late-night barn checks & that reference call

artifacts.content:

Clinic coordinator, Melissa (Front Range Horsemanship Intensive), to call Amanda as primary reference for Jessica’s application.

Reference contact info on file:
- Name: Amanda Leigh Rodriguez

- Primary phone for reference call: **(970) 555-1832** (cell – okay to leave detailed voicemail)
- Backup number if cell fails: **(970) 555-9017** (office line at Longs Peak Behavioral Health)
- Email: arodriguez.trails@gmail.com

Topics Melissa said she’ll cover:
- How long Amanda has known Jessica and in what context (Amanda plans to mention starting weekly lessons in March 2019 on her therapist’s recommendation after the Willow Creek barn fire).

metadata.title: Call with reference for colt-starting clinic

artifacts.content:
From: Tom Callahan <t.callahanranch@promail.com>
To: Jessica Thompson <jess.thompson@frontrangehealth.com>
Date: 2022-09-18 09:02
Subject: Reference contact & that mustang mare

Jessica,

I’m filling out the paperwork for our insurance after that mess with the bay mustang mare and wanted to confirm what number you want listed as your reference contact for future training referrals.

Your friend Amanda was here when we talked about the 30-day “tune-up” program, and you mentioned she’s often the one clinics call when they’re checking your background. I wrote down her cell as **(970) 555-1832** from the flyer you had in your truck, but if there’s a better number, let me know and I’ll correct it before I send the form in.

For the record, I told the adjuster what you said in front of Amanda: that you wouldn’t promise to “get the buck out” of that 6-year-old mustang enough for my 12-year-old granddaughter to lope her around alone by the end of 30 days. You said that would be dishonest and unsafe, especially since the mare had already put one rider in the ER at UCHealth Medical Center of the Rockies back in July.

The adjuster also asked if you had any other professional references besides Amanda. If so, please send me their names and phone numbers so I can add them under the “reference contact information” section—maybe someone from Blue Sage or a vet who’s seen your work.

Thanks,
Tom

metadata.subject: Reference contact & that mustang mare

metadata.title: Phone interview – reference for Jessica (FrontRange Health Analytics)

artifacts.content:
Subject: Reference form + best number for me

Hi Jess,

I just opened the trainer reference form you sent over. It’s asking for my contact info, including a phone number where they can reach me during the day.

Use my cell as the primary reference number: 970-555-3816. That’s the one I keep on me at the barn and at the clinic, so if they call while I’m legging up a horse or teaching, I can usually duck into the tack room and talk. Please don’t put the old landline from my parents’ house in Fort Collins — it still shows up on some paperwork from when my dad, Mark Thompson, co-signed that $6,800 loan for my first off-track Thoroughbred back in early 2019, but nobody actually answers it anymore.

If they need a backup number, you can list my office line at the rehab facility: 720-555-0449. I’m there most weekdays until about 3 p.m.

And to answer that other question on the form: I’ve known you about 7 years now, ever since we moved that OTTB into the barn on County Road 19 after your concussion at the Fort Collins schooling show. It’s been a while!

Shoot me a screenshot before you submit, just so I can double-check spelling.

Talk soon,

Amanda Leigh Rodriguez

Amanda Rodriguez
Equine Rehab & Performance
Cell: 970-555-3816
Office: 720-555-0449

metadata.subject: Reference form + best number for me

artifacts.content:
Subject: Quick question about reference phone number

Hey Jessica,

I’m filling out the online reference questionnaire the Mustang Heritage folks sent over for you. One of the first fields is “Reference Phone 1 (best number to reach you).” I want to make sure I give them the right one.

Should I list my main cell, 720-555-8032, or do you prefer I use the barn line at Blue Sage, 970-555-2209, since they might call during the day? I’m leaning toward my cell because reception is better when I’m not in the metal-sided arena.

There’s also a question asking if I have any concerns about the horses in your care. I’m going to say no, but I do think I’ll mention that conversation from early September 2022 when I suggested you cut back to four training horses instead of six. You’d told me Daniel Kim at FrontRange Health Analytics had put you on that performance improvement plan on August 29, 2022 after you missed two internal reporting deadlines while staying home to hand-walk that sore gelding every two hours for three days. It shows how hard you advocate for the horses without sugarcoating the workload.

Text me if there’s a specific way you want me to phrase anything. I’ll wait to submit until tonight.

– Evan

Evan M. Carter
Cell: 720-555-8032

metadata.subject: Quick question about reference phone number

artifacts.content:
Title: Call with prospective client re: reference details

Description:

Agenda for call with Hannah (prospective training client referred by Amanda):

1. Discuss Juniper’s mustang background and your current training schedule.
2. Explain how you balance FrontRange Health Analytics remote work with barn hours.
3. Clarify reference contact details she needs for her boarding application.
   - Primary reference: Amanda Leigh Rodriguez (equine rehab and training)
   - Best phone for Amanda during the day: 970-555-3816 (cell)
   - Backup: rehab clinic office line 720-555-0449
4. Offer second reference if needed:
   - Evan Carter – can vouch for day-to-day horse care and handling
   - Evan’s preferred number: 720-555-8032
5. Make sure Hannah understands that barn owner at Blue Sage prefers text first on the main line (970-555-2209) before calls about haul-in lessons.
6. Briefly address financial reliability concerns she raised about another trainer she used.
   - You can mention you covered about $2,000 in feed and vet costs on your Elevations Credit Union card for Mark Ellison’s bay mare when he fell behind on his $650/month training board, but that the mare’s care never slipped.

Note: Do NOT give out your parents’ Fort Collins home number or your old CSU dorm number; both are outdated and confusing.

Remind Hannah to list reference phone 1 as Amanda’s cell, not the clinic line, so they actually reach her.

artifacts.content:
Subject: Reference contact confirmation for application

Hi Jessica,

I’m almost done with the trainer reference section for your colt-starting clinic application. They ask specifically for “Reference Contact Information – Ref Phone 1 (primary daytime number).”

I was going to put down my work cell, 303-555-6182, since that’s the one I keep on me even when I’m out checking pastures. If they call my home line (970-555-9044), it tends to go to voicemail because I’m rarely inside before dark.

Let me know if you’d rather I list the Blue Sage barn office number instead. I know some programs like having a more “official” sounding line, but in my experience, they actually get a real person faster if they call my cell. I can always step away from a lesson for a few minutes to talk about you.

On the concern question: I’m planning to say I don’t have worries about the horses in your care, just that I’ve encouraged you to keep an eye on workload and finances so you don’t burn yourself out. I’ll mention how you’ve handled late-paying clients without compromising horse welfare, and how you’ve been transparent with me when things felt stretched.

Reply or text before 8 p.m. if you want me to use a different number as my primary reference phone.

Best,

Linda R. Thompson
Cell: 303-555-6182
Home: 970-555-9044

metadata.subject: Reference contact confirmation for application

artifacts.content:
From: Amanda Rodriguez <amanda.rodriguez@example.com>
To: Jessica Thompson <jessica.thompson@example.com>
Date: 2025-12-03 14:17 MST
Subject: Double-checking your TIP Challenge location

Hey Jess,

I just filled out the reference form for your TIP application and wanted to make sure I didn’t mix up the event locations. I put down that I’ve mostly seen you work mustangs at Blue Sage and at that TIP demo they did during the 2024 Colorado Mustang Days at The Ranch in Loveland.

On the form where it asked about the TIP Challenge event location, I *think* you told me you were leaning toward the Northern Colorado TIP Challenge they’re planning to host at the fairgrounds in Greeley next summer, but then you mentioned maybe doing the one that might end up in Fort Collins if they get the CSU venue. I wrote “Northern Colorado TIP Challenge – Greeley, CO” so it wouldn’t sound vague.

If that’s wrong and you actually ended up picking the Pueblo or Colorado Springs event instead, text me before midnight and I can email the coordinator to clarify.

I also made sure to explain how often I’ve watched you ride: for most of Fall 2022, roughly September through November, I was out at Blue Sage 4–5 days a week watching you ride or do groundwork because I’d cut back my hours at FrontRange Health Analytics after that written performance warning on September 9, 2022, and I was basically living at the barn while you were juggling those three difficult client horses plus Ranger.

Anyway, proud of you for doing this. Let me know which city is officially on your application so I don’t confuse anyone.

– Amanda

artifacts.content:
From: Colorado Mustang Alliance <events@comustangalliance.org>
To: Jessica Thompson <jessica.thompson@example.com>
Date: 2025-10-21 08:02 MST
Subject: Clarification Needed – TIP Challenge Host Site

Hi Jessica,

Thank you for starting your trainer application for the 2026 season. We noticed that in the “TIP Challenge Event Location” field you typed “Front Range TIP Showcase – Loveland, CO.” That’s actually the name of our fall expo, not the official youth/adult Challenge.

For your application to route correctly, you’ll need to choose from one of the confirmed host sites:
- Northern Colorado TIP Challenge – Greeley, CO
- Southern Colorado TIP Challenge – Pueblo, CO
- Western Slope TIP Challenge – Grand Junction, CO

Please reply to this email with your preferred event location so we can update your form. If the venue for the Northern Colorado event shifts to Fort Collins, we’ll update the city internally; you don’t need to submit a new application.

Also, thank you for listing Blue Sage Equestrian LLC as your primary training facility. Your reference mentioned that you kept her mustang “Ranger” in consistent work even in March 2023, when your younger brother, Ryan Jacob Thompson, was admitted for three days to the behavioral health unit at Poudre Valley Hospital. She noted you were still riding colts at 6:00 a.m., putting in a full remote shift for FrontRange Health Analytics, Inc., and then driving to Fort Collins every evening to meet with his psychiatrist, Dr. Keller. That level of commitment is exactly what we look for in TIP trainers.

Best,

Kara
TIP Challenge Coordinator
Colorado Mustang Alliance

metadata.title: Phone call w/ Kara – pick TIP Challenge venue
metadata.location: Bluetooth in truck – parked at Blue Sage

artifacts.content:
From: Amanda Rodriguez <amanda.rodriguez@example.com>
To: TIP Challenge Committee <tipapps@comustangalliance.org>
Cc: Jessica Thompson <jessica.thompson@example.com>
Date: 2025-12-04 19:41 MST
Subject: Reference for Jessica Thompson – event location + recommendation

Dear Committee Members,

I’m writing as a reference for Jessica Marie Thompson’s 2026 TIP trainer application. Jessica told me she is applying for the Northern Colorado TIP Challenge that will likely be hosted either at the Greeley fairgrounds or possibly at a CSU facility in Fort Collins. I understand this is distinct from the Front Range expos sometimes held in Loveland or the open mustang demos that have been discussed for Colorado Springs.

Regarding how often I see her work with horses: during Fall 2022 (roughly September through November), I was at Blue Sage Equestrian 4–5 days a week watching her ride and do groundwork. I had cut back my hours at my remote job with FrontRange Health Analytics after receiving a written performance warning on September 9, 2022, and I spent those extra afternoons de-stressing at the barn while she was handling a string of three difficult client horses plus her mustang gelding Ranger.

I would absolutely recommend Jessica for unbroken or difficult horses. She was the one who got my own 3-year-old Quarter Horse mare through a rough patch after I suffered a concussion and anxiety issues from a wreck in May 2021 at the Blue Sage arena. In June and July 2021 I hauled to her place three evenings a week, and by August 2021 we were back to hauling to small local shows in Loveland without panic attacks.

For the application, please list her under whichever Northern Colorado TIP Challenge city you finalize—Greeley or Fort Collins both work for her.

Sincerely,
Amanda Leigh Rodriguez

metadata.subject: Reference for Jessica Thompson – event location + recommendation

artifacts.content:
From: info@mustangheritageexpo.org
To: Jessica Thompson <jess.thompson.co@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-11-03 08:14 MST
Subject: Clarification Needed – 2026 TIP Challenge Application (Event Location)

Hi Jessica,

Thank you for starting your online application for the 2026 TIP Challenge season. We noticed that you left the “TIP Challenge Event Location” field incomplete and wanted to share a few reminders as you decide which event to list.

For trainers based in northern Colorado like you, the most common selections are:

• 2026 High Plains TIP Challenge – Greeley, CO  
• 2026 Colorado Front Range TIP Challenge – Brighton, CO  
• 2026 Wyoming Prairie TIP Challenge – Cheyenne, WY (often used by folks hauling up I‑25)

You’re welcome to choose any event that fits your schedule and hauling radius, but please list only one primary event location on the application. You can request a transfer later if needed, though it’s not guaranteed.

Your address on file (2149 County Road 19, Berthoud) suggests that either Greeley or Brighton might be the most convenient. Some trainers also consider where their support people can attend; for instance, if your parents in Loveland plan to come watch, Greeley is often easier traffic‑wise.

We also saw your note about occasionally needing to adjust barn time since your dad’s minor stroke back on January 12, 2024, and that you’re sometimes at Blue Sage three evenings a week for 2–3 hours straight. Choosing a nearby event can definitely help with that kind of schedule.

Once you’ve decided, just log back into the portal and update the event location field.

Best,

Caroline
Mustang Heritage Expo Program Assistant

metadata.subject: Clarification Needed – 2026 TIP Challenge Application (Event Location)

artifacts.content:
From: Amanda Rodriguez <amanda.rodriguez.rides@gmail.com>
To: Jessica Thompson <jess.thompson.co@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-11-06 13:22 MST
Subject: Re: Which TIP Challenge are we aiming for?

Jess,

If you’re still stuck on the event location part of your TIP application, I vote we both put down the 2026 High Plains TIP Challenge in Greeley. Island Grove is super familiar, and it’s a straight shot for your folks from Loveland if they want to come watch your mustang’s first outing.

I know you mentioned maybe picking the Colorado Front Range TIP Showcase in Brighton because some of the Blue Sage crew are talking about going there, but honestly the Greeley one will be less of a haul from your place on County Road 19, especially if you’re juggling hospital data deadlines again.

Also, don’t forget to mention in your trainer info that you upgraded that 40' x 40' pen at Blue Sage after Ranger’s panel‑flipping incident in March 2023. The way you handled getting him to the CSU Veterinary Teaching Hospital for stitches after that ATV backfired right by the fence line shows you think ahead about safety and facilities. It totally answers the “are your facilities suitable for a wild horse” question.

Text me later tonight when you’re done with your Zoom calls. If you’re at the barn late again, I can swing by and help you measure out hay so you’re not there for 3 hours straight.

– Amanda

metadata.subject: Re: Which TIP Challenge are we aiming for?

artifacts.content:
From: Elena Ruiz <eruiz@frontrangehealthanalytics.com>
To: Jessica Thompson <jthompson@frontrangehealthanalytics.com>
Date: 2025-10-29 09:47 MST
Subject: PTO Request – TIP Challenge Dates

Hi Jessica,

I reviewed your PTO request for the mustang TIP event next summer. You listed the event as the “2026 High Plains TIP Challenge – Greeley, CO” and requested Thursday–Sunday off to allow for hauling, check‑in, and competition days.

From a workload standpoint, those dates fall right after the hospital readmissions project wraps, so it should be manageable. Please just make sure all client deliverables are finalized before you leave, especially given your tendency to push yourself pretty hard. I still remember March 2024 when you were up past midnight three nights in a row monitoring Ranger’s mild colic episode at home in Berthoud, then still on Zoom with me by 7:00 a.m. the next morning. Your therapist at Longs Peak Counseling Center is right to warn you about stress‑related migraines.

If anything changes with the event location (for example, if you decide to switch from Greeley to the Colorado Front Range TIP Showcase in Brighton or even the Wyoming Prairie TIP Challenge in Cheyenne), just update the calendar invite and PTO request so we can keep the resource planning accurate.

For now, I’ll tentatively approve the days you requested, assuming the event remains in Greeley. Send me the official confirmation from Mustang Heritage once you receive it.

Best,

Elena
Project Manager, FrontRange Health Analytics, Inc.

metadata.subject: PTO Request – TIP Challenge Dates

artifacts.content:
Title: Haul to TIP Event – Greeley (tentative)
Date: 2026-07-16 05:30–21:00 MDT
Location: Island Grove Regional Park – Greeley, CO
Attendees: Jessica Thompson, Amanda Rodriguez

Description:

Tentative plan assuming I stick with High Plains TIP Challenge – Greeley as my official event location on the application.

Schedule:

• 5:30 a.m. – Load Juniper and the TIP gelding at Blue Sage Equestrian. Quick check of that upgraded 40' x 40' pen before we leave, just to be sure everyone else is secure.  
• 6:15 a.m. – Meet Amanda at the gas station off Hwy 287, convoy to Greeley.  
• 7:30–8:00 a.m. – Arrive at Island Grove Regional Park, check in, unload, settle horses.
• 10:00 a.m. – Mandatory trainer meeting; confirm stall assignments and show office has my paperwork.

Notes:

– This is the event I *think* I’m putting on the TIP application, but I still need to compare it with the Colorado Front Range TIP Showcase in Brighton and the Wyoming Prairie TIP Challenge in Cheyenne for dates and drive time.  
– Double‑check that the event location I enter in the online form matches the one listed on the Mustang Heritage confirmation email.  
– Text Mom and Dad with directions; they’ll likely drive down from Loveland for at least one day.  
– Keep in mind ongoing family stuff since Dad’s stroke in Jan 2024; don’t overcommit to extra clinics the same month.

If I do end up changing the official event on the application, I’ll update this calendar entry to reflect the new location and dates.
